summary: Re-linked the menu (`mn`) library slice by regenerating `asm/melee/mn/mncharsel.s` as a relocatable-style assembly file and moving `mn_2295.c`/`mncharsel.c` from `Unknown` to `NonMatching` in `configure.py`. The PR also cleaned up menu headers by splitting shared menu data declarations into new `mn/forward.h` and `mn/types.h`, shrinking `mncharsel.h` to just public declarations, deleting the empty `mntext.h`, and adding an empty guarded `mndeflicker.h` stub.

key_files.role: Large regenerated/re-linked assembly file; now begins with `.file "mncharsel.c"`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, and `.fn ... global` style function annotations instead of the prior absolute-address `.section .text # 0x8025C020 - ...` form.
Changed `melee/mn_2295.c` and `melee/mn/mncharsel.c` from `Object(Unknown, ...)` to `Object(NonMatching, ...)` under the `mn (Menus)` library.
Removed `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c.o` from `TEXT_O_FILES`, leaving the assembly object for `mncharsel.s` in the link list.
Reduced from a mixed declarations/types header to a small public header with GALE-style guard and two address-commented declarations.
New shared menu type-definition header containing `PlayerInitData`, `StartMeleeRules`, `StartMeleeData`, `VsModeData`, and `CSSData`.
New forward-declaration header for menu structs and enums `Mode` and `StageSelectMode`.
Now includes `mn/types.h`, reflecting the type split out of `mncharsel.h`.
Header guard changed to GALE address-style `GALE01_2295AC`; declaration annotated with address comment.
New empty guarded header stub using GALE address-style guard `GALE01_24A168`.
Header guard changed from descriptive path-style guard to GALE address-style `GALE01_0E5CB0`.

what_changed: Marked `melee/mn_2295.c` and `melee/mn/mncharsel.c` as `NonMatching` instead of `Unknown` in `configure.py`, making them part of the tracked decompilation state even though not fully matching.
Removed the C object for `mncharsel.c` from `obj_files.mk` while retaining `asm/melee/mn/mncharsel.s.o`, indicating the link still uses assembly for that translation unit.

Replaced the old absolute-address `mncharsel.s` assembly listing with a re-linked form using `.file "mncharsel.c"`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, `.fn`, `.endfn`, local `.L_000...` labels, and zero-based code offsets in comments.
Moved menu data structures and enums out of `mncharsel.h` into new `mn/forward.h` and `mn/types.h` headers.

Changed `mncharsel.h` from a 158-line mixed type/declaration header into a minimal public declaration header with only `TextKerning* mnCharSel_8025BC20(TextKerning* arg0, u32 arg1);` and `void mnCharSel_8025BD30(void);`.
Added address comments before public declarations, for example `/* 25BC20 */` in `mncharsel.h` and `/* 22F3D8 */` in `mn_2295.h`.
Deleted `src/melee/mn/mntext.h`, which only contained an include guard and broad includes.
Added an empty `mndeflicker.h` stub with a GALE address-style include guard.
Updated several include guards from descriptive or lowercase forms to GALE address-style names, including `GALE01_25BC20`, `GALE01_2295AC`, and `GALE01_0E5CB0`.
smart_moves: Separated forward declarations from full type definitions, letting headers include lighter dependencies when only opaque menu types or enums are needed.
Centralized shared menu structs in `mn/types.h` instead of keeping large gameplay/menu data layouts inside `mncharsel.h`, reducing accidental coupling to the character-select module.
Used `#include "mn/forward.h" // IWYU pragma: export` in `types.h`, making the type header also provide the forward declarations intentionally.
Kept `mncharsel.c` present and tracked as `NonMatching` while linking the assembly object, preserving decompilation progress without pretending the C matches.
Annotated declarations with function addresses, which helps future symbol lookup and cross-reference work.
Normalized header guards to the project's GALE address-style convention in touched headers.
decomp_lessons: For large relink PRs, first stabilize object/build metadata: move files from `Unknown` to `NonMatching` only when they are being tracked, and ensure the actual linked object still comes from asm if the C is not ready.

When a module header accumulates shared data layouts, split reusable structs into a `types.h` and opaque names/enums into a `forward.h`; keep the module header focused on that module's public functions.

A generated/relinked asm file may intentionally lose original absolute-address section comments and instead use zero-based offsets plus `.fn`/`.endfn` metadata; future reviews should distinguish this from a semantic rewrite.
Do not keep empty placeholder headers with broad includes if they add no declarations; deleting them can reduce include noise.
Address-commented declarations are useful in nonmatching areas where symbol names are still address-derived and future cleanup depends on fast lookup.
naming_conventions: Header guards in touched module headers use GALE address-style names, e.g. `GALE01_25BC20`, `GALE01_2295AC`, `GALE01_24A168`, and `GALE01_0E5CB0`.
Shared menu opaque types are named without leading underscores in typedefs: `CSSData`, `PlayerInitData`, `StartMeleeData`, `StartMeleeRules`, and `VsModeData`.

Struct definitions in `types.h` use `struct PlayerInitData` style paired with typedefs from `forward.h`, rather than the old `typedef struct _PlayerInitData { ... } PlayerInitData;` pattern in `mncharsel.h`.
Public declarations retain existing address-derived names such as `mnCharSel_8025BC20`, `mnCharSel_8025BD30`, and `mn_8022F3D8`.
Function prototypes in touched headers are prefixed with address comments like `/* 25BC20 */` and `/* 22F3D8 */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted `mncharsel.s` into a relinkable-style assembly file with `.file "mncharsel.c"`, `.text`, `.balign 4`, and `.fn name, global` annotations.
The new assembly comments use zero-based offsets such as `/* 00000000 00000040 ... */` rather than original RAM addresses such as `/* 8025C020 ... */`, matching a re-linked object workflow.
Kept branch/call sites as relocatable placeholder calls such as `bl HSD_SisLib_803A6530` with encoded placeholder bytes in the diff, instead of relying only on fixed absolute branch displacements.
Removed the `src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c.o` text object while retaining the `asm/melee/mn/mncharsel.s.o` object, allowing a nonmatching C file to coexist with asm-linked output.
Marking `mncharsel.c` and `mn_2295.c` as `NonMatching` in `configure.py` documents that decompilation work exists but exact matching is not complete.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert `melee/mn/mncharsel.c` from `NonMatching` to matching.
Search for later uses of `mn/types.h` and `mn/forward.h` to see whether the type split became the standard menu-header pattern.
Check whether `mndeflicker.h` later gains declarations after this empty guarded stub.
Review subsequent relink PRs for the same `.file`, `.fn`, zero-offset assembly style used here.
Search for cleanup PRs that rename address-derived `mnCharSel_*` symbols after this relink.
